rulings.Case Title: MARK BIRKHEAD VS. OLIVER PARKER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default And Order;
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 6, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF MARK BIRKHEAD's Motion To Set Aside Default And Order. Continued to June 17, 2014 to give Mr. Birkhead the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Mr. Birkhead must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than June 5, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANE FENG VS. DAVID YANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn Of Stay In Supporting The Points Of The Plaintiffs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 6, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFFS JANE FENG AND ZHEN ZHOU'S Motion Of Stay In Supporting The Points Of The Plaintiffs Motion denied. While the nature of the motion brought by Plaintiffs and the relief sought thereby is unclear, the motion asserts no valid grounds for relief from the Order Granting Defendant's Motion for Summary Judgment, which was issued and entered on January 29, 2014 and of which Plaintiff was served with notice on January 30, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: HEYDARI, SAMANTHA, BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD et al VS. TERENCE EMMET WARD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 6, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF SAMANTHA HEYDARI, BY AND THROUGH HER GUARDIAN AD LITEM, MATTHEW HEYDARI, BY AND THROUGH HIS GUARDIAN AD LITEM, MEL HEYDARI'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Parties to Appear. Parties to meet and confer prior to the hearing, and inform the Court if Defendant¿s Second Amended Responses to Form Interrogatory 17.1 has been served, and if further discovery issues remain. Plaintiff¿s Request for Sanctions is DENIED. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE FINLEY VS. DREXEL A. BRADSHAW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 6, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTs DREXEL BRADSHAW and BRADSHAW AND ASSOCIATES, P.C's motion For Sanctions Denied. There is no evidence that the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") was filed for an improper purpose. The claims are not frivolous. As stated in the ruling on the demurrers to the FAC, Plaintiff may be able to amend her complaint to state a cause of action against Defendants. Plaintiff has provided evidence showing that she is likely to have evidentiary support for her claims. (Declaration of Daniel Berko.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/eg

rulings.Case Title: RISA CORWIN et al VS. MICHAEL SPAIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Of Defendant Michael Spain'S Motion To Compel Bridget Mckenna, Ph.D To Comply With Subpoena For Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, June 6, 2014, Line 4, Hearing Of Defendant Michael Spain's Motion To Compel Bridget Mckenna, Ph.D To Comply With Subpoena For Business Records And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Rebecca Woodson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant MICHAEL SPAIN¿s Motion to Compel is DENIED. Dr. Bridget McKenna has executed an Affidavit of No Records on April 28, 2014 and an Affidavit of Custodian of Records on May 1, 2014. Defendant¿s Request for Sanctions is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rwoodson@mckennalong.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILLIAM S WEISBERG et al VS. HEIDI TIMKEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 6, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTS HEIDI TIMKEN, LESLIE JOHNSON, CHRISTOPHER GONZALEZ AND TIMKEN JOHNSON, LLP'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The motion is granted. The malicious prosecution cause of action implicates CCP 425.16. Plaintiff is therefore obliged to present evidence to establish the malicious prosecution cause of action. Plaintiff fails to present sufficient evidence showing that the defendants acted with malice. At most, plaintiff has merely shown a lack of probable cause and that alone does not support a finding of malice. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/EG
